summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates npm tokens and environment to hardcoded remote IP
details: On install, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that collects the output of `id`, `env | sort`, `/proc/1/environ`, the contents of every `.npmrc` file on the filesystem (npm auth tokens), and `env | grep -iE 'FLAG|HTB|SECRET|TOKEN|PASSWORD'`. The collected data is base64-wrapped in a fake YAML manifest and HTTP PUT to `http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/<sink>`. A `shouldRun()` gate in postinstall.js suppresses execution under common registry mirrors (npmmirror, tencent, hscan, ustc, huawei, cnpm, taobao) and CI environments (GITHUB_ACTIONS, RUNNER_OS, CI), and only fires when the hostname is 12 hex characters and the cwd contains `/app` — a container/CTF fingerprint designed to evade automated review while still firing on real victim hosts. The combination of credential theft (.npmrc tokens, credential-shaped env vars, pid 1 environment), hardcoded plain-HTTP exfiltration to a public IP, and explicit scanner-evasion gating is an unambiguous supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
